explanation: The natural language statement "The system shall measure the Ventilatorv0.2 pressure at/near the inlet to the patient" directly translates to the atomic proposition "measurePSins". The term "shall measure" implies a continuous and mandatory requirement for the system to perform this action. Therefore, the LTL operator 'G' (globally) is used to ensure that this condition holds true at all times.

The natural language statement "The system shall measure the Ventilatorv0.2 pressure at/near the inlet to the patient" indicates a continuous and mandatory requirement for the system to perform this measurement. The atomic proposition "measurePSins" directly corresponds to "measurement of the Ventilatorv0.2 pressure at/near the inlet to the patient". To express that this measurement 'shall' always be taking place, the Global (G) operator is used. Thus, 'G measurePSins' means that the system must always measure the Ventilatorv0.2 pressure at/near the inlet to the patient.

The atomic proposition 'measurePSins' directly represents this measurement. Therefore, G(measurePSins) states that it is globally true that the system measures the pressure at the inlet to the patient.
